text: EIE Dept – Siddhartha University Skip to content Search Search Home Faculty Faculty Adjunct Faculty Non Teaching Staff Students Achievements & Participations Internships Higher Studies Student Publications ISOI & Spandana club Activities Innovative models R & D Publications Patents Awards Projects Placements Events Infrastructure Updates Magazines Home Faculty Faculty Adjunct Faculty Non Teaching Staff Students Achievements & Participations Internships Higher Studies Student Publications ISOI & Spandana club Activities Innovative models R & D Publications Patents Awards Projects Placements Events Infrastructure Updates Magazines About The Department The department was started in 1995 as Instrumentation Engineering. Later it was renamed as Electronics and Instrumentation Engineering in 2003 with an intake of 120 students. Since its inception, the department has been committed to excellence in teaching, innovation, and research. With a team of 22 dedicated faculty members, most of whom are highly experienced and specialized in various domains of instrumentation and automation, the department provides strong academic and professional mentorship. The department is equipped with advanced laboratories, including both state-of-the-art facilities and industry-collaborative labs, which enable students to gain hands-on experience aligned with current technological trends and industrial practices. Our focus remains on nurturing technically competent, industry-ready graduates who can contribute meaningfully to the fields of Process Control, Automation, Embedded Systems, and Industrial Instrumentation. Vision To provide high-quality education in Electronics and Instrumentation Engineering through a curriculum grounded in scientific knowledge and modern technology, to foster strong analytical and problem-solving skills for addressing real-world challenges in industry, healthcare, and automation. To encourage innovation, research, and interdisciplinary collaboration through partnerships with industries, research institutions, and global academic bodies. To cultivate professional, ethical, and socially responsible engineers who can lead, adapt, and thrive in a rapidly changing technological landscape. To empower students with lifelong learning abilities, leadership qualities, and entrepreneurial mind set in alignment with the university’s commitment to knowledge creation and societal impact. Mission Dr.G.N.Swamy Professor & Head Welcome Message Welcome to the Department of Electronics and Instrumentation Engineering. Since its establishment in 2003, the department has been committed to academic excellence, industry relevance, and holistic student development. With a team of experienced faculty and a blend of advanced and industry-collaborative laboratories, we provide a strong foundation in instrumentation, automation, and emerging technologies. Our goal is to nurture competent, ethical, and industry-ready professionals who can meet the evolving challenges of the engineering world. Programs Offered Electronics and Instrumentation Engineering Year of Establishment: 1995 Intake: 120 Placements The Department of Electronics and Instrumentation Engineering (EIE) is recognized as a promising hub for recruitment by reputed companies. We are committed to nurturing students into well-rounded professionals by combining strong theoretical knowledge with practical skills, along with focused training in soft skills and industry-oriented practices to enhance employability. These continuous efforts have led to commendable placement outcomes, with many of our students securing campus placements every year, reflecting the department’s consistent emphasis on academic excellence and career readiness. Placement Offers 0 Companies Visited 0 HIghest Package 0 LPA Average CTC 0 LPA Package offered by Reliance for 11 Students 0 LPA No. of Students placed in Core Industries 0 Placement Report Faculty The Department of Electronics and Instrumentation Engineering (EIE) takes pride in its highly experienced and dedicated faculty, with 68% of the faculty holding Ph.D. degrees from reputed institutions. This strong academic foundation enables them to effectively impart both theoretical knowledge and practical skills to students. The faculty actively contribute to research through publications in reputed international journals and by securing patents for their innovative work. They regularly participate in international conferences, faculty development programs (FDPs), and workshops to stay updated with emerging technologies and industry trends. Furthermore, strong collaborations with industry enhance practical exposure and promote innovation. Committed to excellence in teaching and research, the faculty play a vital role in shaping students into competent professionals and future innovators.
